channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: AraModernBERT: Transtokenized Initialization and Long-Context Encoder Modeling for Arabic
item.description: arXiv:2603.09982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Encoder-only transformer models remain widely used for discriminative NLP tasks, yet recent architectural advances have largely focused on English. In this work, we present AraModernBERT, an adaptation of the ModernBERT encoder architecture to Arabic,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, context, arxiv, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models: An Empirical Study of Confidence Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.09985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet their ability to accurately assess their own confidence remains poorly understood. We present an empirical study investigating whether LLMs exhibit patter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, study, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Causally Grounded Mechanistic Interpretability for LLMs with Faithful Natural-Language Explanations
item.description: arXiv:2603.09988v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mechanistic interpretability identifies internal circuits responsible for model behaviors, yet translating these findings into human-understandable explanations remains an open problem. We present a pipeline that bridges circuit-level analysis and nat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, template, arxiv, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Two-Stage Architecture for NDA Analysis: LLM-based Segmentation and Transformer-based Clause Classification
item.description: arXiv:2603.09990v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In business-to-business relations, it is common to establish NonDisclosure Agreements (NDAs). However, these documents exhibit significant variation in format, structure, and writing style, making manual analysis slow and error-prone. We propose an ar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, transformer, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Resource-constrained Amazons chess decision framework integrating large language models and graph attention
item.description: arXiv:2603.10512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence has advanced significantly through the development of intelligent game-playing systems, providing rigorous testbeds for decision-making, strategic planning, and adaptive learning. However, resource-constrained environments pose...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, vision, arxiv, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Adaptive RAN Slicing Control via Reward-Free Self-Finetuning Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10564v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Generative AI models into AI-native network systems offers a transformative path toward achieving autonomous and adaptive control. However, the application of such models to continuous control tasks is impeded by intrinsic architect...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, fine-tuning, arxiv, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Does LLM Alignment Really Need Diversity? An Empirical Study of Adapting RLVR Methods for Moral Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable success in logical reasoning tasks, yet whether large language model (LLM) alignment requires fundamentally different approaches remains unclear. Given the apparent toleranc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, study, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MoE-SpAc: Efficient MoE Inference Based on Speculative Activation Utility in Heterogeneous Edge Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2603.09983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models enable scalable performance but face severe memory constraints on edge devices. Existing offloading strategies struggle with I/O bottlenecks due to the dynamic, low-information nature of autoregressive expert activation...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, arxiv, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LWM-Temporal: Sparse Spatio-Temporal Attention for Wireless Channel Representation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LWM-Temporal is a new member of the Large Wireless Models (LWM) family that targets the spatiotemporal nature of wireless channels. Designed as a task-agnostic foundation model, LWM-Temporal learns universal channel embeddings that capture mobility-in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, fine-tuning, arxiv, experiment, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Revisiting Sharpness-Aware Minimization: A More Faithful and Effective Implementation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) enhances generalization by minimizing the maximum training loss within a predefined neighborhood around the parameters. However, its practical implementation approximates this as gradient ascent(s) followed by applyi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, RAG, arxiv, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: InFusionLayer: a CFA-based ensemble tool to generate new classifiers for learning and modeling
item.description: arXiv:2603.10049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ensemble learning is a well established body of methods for machine learning to enhance predictive performance by combining multiple algorithms/models. Combinatorial Fusion Analysis (CFA) has provided method and practice for combining multiple scoring...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, tool, analysis, model, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cluster-Aware Attention-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning for Pickup and Delivery Problems
item.description: arXiv:2603.10053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Pickup and Delivery Problem (PDP) is a fundamental and challenging variant of the Vehicle Routing Problem, characterized by tightly coupled pickup--delivery pairs, precedence constraints, and spatial layouts that often exhibit clustering. Existing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, arxiv, ICL, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HTMuon: Improving Muon via Heavy-Tailed Spectral Correction
item.description: arXiv:2603.10067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Muon has recently shown promising results in LLM training. In this work, we study how to further improve Muon. We argue that Muon's orthogonalized update rule suppresses the emergence of heavy-tailed weight spectra and over-emphasizes the training alo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, analysis, arxiv, experiment, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Quantifying Hallucinations in Language Language Models on Medical Textbooks
item.description: arXiv:2603.09986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hallucinations, the tendency for large language models to provide responses with factually incorrect and unsupported claims, is a serious problem within natural language processing for which we do not yet have an effective solution to mitigate against...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment, prompt, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distilling reasoning capabilities from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into smaller models is typically constrained by the limitation of rejection sampling. Standard methods treat the teacher as a static filter, discarding complex "corner-case" problems...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CUAAudit: Meta-Evaluation of Vision-Language Models as Auditors of Autonomous Computer-Use Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) are emerging as a new paradigm in human-computer interaction, enabling autonomous execution of tasks in desktop environment by perceiving high-level natural-language instructions. As such agents become increasingly capable a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, vision, instruction, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Gated Adaptation for Continual Learning in Human Activity Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.10046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wearable sensors in Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems increasingly support applications such as remote health monitoring, elderly care, and smart home automation, all of which rely on robust human activity recognition (HAR). Continual learning syste...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
